title: Tutoring Services * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Tutoring Services * Native American Community 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Autism Spectrum Disorder Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LANE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CHIFIN NATIVE YOUTH CENTER INDIAN EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers learning, leadership, and cultural activities that engage Native youth and support a positive cultural identity. Provides culturally appropriate educational support to Native students, including tutoring and credit recovery, and supports Native families’ involvement in students’ education. 1084 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Native American and Alaskan Native students and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 726-4301 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an advocacy program that helps students and their families set and achieve goals in four key areas: Mind, Body, Spirit, and Context. Services include wrap-around services, advocacy services, college visits, life skills workshops, job training, financial aid assistance, school tutoring, after-school homework help, and the development of cultural pride and relevant skills. Incentives like stipends, meals, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and financial assistance for youth participants. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Portland Public School District: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth -Successful Families 2020: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within David Douglas and Reynolds school districts. -SUN Youth Advocacy: 5th-10th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within Centennial, David Douglas, Gresham-Barlow, Parkrose, Portland Public, and Reynolds school districts. OR Region 3: 5th-10th grade any race/ethnicity enrolled in the following schools: Alice Ott Middle, Cherry Park Elementary, Gilbert Heights Elementary, Gilbert Park Elementary, Parkrose High, Parkrose Middle, Prescott Elementary, Sacramento Elementary, and Shaver Elementary Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm, some evening hours for events Email (503) 288-8177 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT INDIAN EDUCATION PROGRAM NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT
